content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN. In one operation, you can:


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N)(1\le i \le N)(1≤i≤N) and increase AiA_iAi​ by 111.
Find the **minimum** number of operations required to convert the array AAA into a permutation of size NNN. If it is impossible to do so, print −1-1−1.

Note that a permutation of size NNN contains each element from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains the integer NNN — the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of operations required to convert the array AAA into a permutation of size NNN.

If it is impossible to do so, print −1-1−1.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of operations required to transform the array into a permutation of size N, or -1 if it is impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Increment the third element three times to obtain [3,1,4,2] – a permutation. Minimum operations = 3.
Test case 2: Element 3 is already > N=3, impossible.
Test case 3: Array is already a permutation.
Test case 4: Increment elements 1,2,3 each once to obtain [3,1,2]. Minimum operations = 3.
Increase the only element from 0 to 1.
The array is already the permutation [1,2,3,4,5].

solution.approach: We can only increase elements. After the transformation each value must be a distinct integer from 1 to N and must be >= its original value. Sort the array. Keep a pointer cur to the smallest still unused number (initially 1). For each element a in the sorted array: set cur = max(cur, a). If cur > N the transformation is impossible. Otherwise assign cur to this element, add (cur - a) to the answer and increment cur. This greedy choice always uses the smallest feasible target for each element, which minimizes the total increments.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case because of sorting.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        if any(x > N for x in a):
            out_lines.append('-1')
            continue
        a.sort()
        cur = 1
        ops = 0
        possible = True
        for x in a:
            if cur < x:
                cur = x
            if cur > N:
                possible = False
                break
            ops += cur - x
            cur += 1
        out_lines.append(str(ops) if possible else '-1')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
